summary: The build failed while running a RUN step in the Dockerfile that downloads the nydus binary; wget returned an HTTP 502 and the build aborted.
root_cause: The Docker build step running wget to fetch the nydus release returned HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway, causing the build to fail with exit code 1.

evidence.quote: wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: Shows the external download request failed with an HTTP 502 error.

evidence.quote: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: BuildKit reports the wget command failed and the solve/build was aborted.
Identifies the specific build step that ran wget to fetch the nydus binary.
Log shows the failing RUN corresponds to line 336 of the Dockerfile (location of the wget).

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: CI runner reports the docker/buildx bake step failed due to the wget failure.

suggested_fix: Make the Dockerfile download step resilient or avoid relying on the public host at build time. Concretely: update the RUN at Dockerfile:336 to retry the download (e.g. wget --tries=5 --waitretry=5 or curl --retry 5), add fallbacks/mirrors, or vend the nydus artifact into a stable cache/registry used by CI so the build doesn't fail on transient network/502 errors; then re-run the job.
